sourceMovie.title: The Orphanage
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Orphanage works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Possum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Orphanage clicked for you, Possum (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hereditary
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Orphanage clicked for you, Hereditary (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Orphanage?
faq.answer: Try Possum, Hereditary, The Others—each shares DNA with The Orphanage while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Orphanage?
faq.answer: The Orphanage (2007) is best described as Horror, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Orphanage worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
